title: Copy paste from Excel Column to Monday Column
excerpt: Good day. I would like to copy a column from excel and paste it into a column in Monday.com. Is there any way to do this?

posts.author: Shell (Shelley Beairsto)
posts.cookedHtml: Good day. I would like to copy a column from excel and paste it into a column in Monday.com. Is there any way to do this?

posts.author: alejencia (Alex Becerra)
posts.cookedHtml: not that I’m aware of. This would be a very helpful function. I’d vote to have this.

posts.author: Shell (Shelley Beairsto)
posts.cookedHtml: let’s ask the team! how do we do that?

posts.author: nealb (Neal Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml:
you have my cote on this

this is the first thing I tried to do when I joined monday, seems that is should be easy since you can copy and paste the pulse names

posts.author: Marisa (Marisa)
posts.cookedHtml: I’ve been able to copy one column from Excel into Monday but when trying to do the same in another column, it pastes all the information into the cell. Would love some help with this task, if anyone can help…

posts.author: hdurey (Hunt Durey)
posts.cookedHtml: @Julia-monday.com please take note of this request and pass along to developers. This is a key (and I assume fairly simple) update to functionality. We work with many Excel spreadsheets and need to be able to bulk copy-paste between Excel tables and Monday.com boards. It’s great that you can bulk paste into item names, but without the ability to also bulk paste into other associated columns like text and numbers, it severely limits the power of using Monday.com and Excel together. It’s also a huge time waster having to copy-paste data 1 field at a time. Thanks. FYI - there is a second feature request thread for this same topic: Copy/Paste into columns of numbers/text.

posts.author: Julia-monday.com (Julia Fagelman)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you @hdurey
I will pass this request along to the relevant domain. Right now though, have you tried the import from Excel feature? I assume you have but wanted to check

posts.author: hdurey (Hunt Durey)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks. Yes, I have used the Excel import extensively. The problem is that I need to be able to bulk-copy-paste Excel data into items (and their associated fields) on existing boards / groups, and that need is not addressed by the Excel import function that can only import data into a new board.

posts.author: brett-monday.com (Brett Dalton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all! Thanks for the feedback on this, I appreciate you taking the time to highlight your current pain-points for this and we see the need. I will be sure to pass this along to our team and have them take a look for planning.

We appreciate the feedback

Cheers!

posts.author: NoChance (Chance)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is one of those things that I just tried to do because I’m used to being able to do it. But it doesn’t work outside the first column. Really decreases efficiency for me.

Hope you will consider adding this. Thanks!

posts.cookedHtml: any updates to this?

Is there any update on that? @mon

posts.author: MSIlydia (Lydia Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a big deal for my boss. It’s also a big deal for trying to bring in large spreadsheets that have too many items or too many columns…if we could copy and paste columns, we could bring in the information more efficiently than writing third party code to bring the information in.

posts.cookedHtml: Hi there, I vote also for this kind of feature. very frustrating so far

posts.author: Blazej (Blazej Jedras)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes please. So frustrating to be sitting here, manually typing in data from 124 rows into 124 Monday items
Any dev response would be most welcome.

posts.author: AlexStanhope (Alex Stanhope)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Julia-monday.com
Its crazy that you cant do this either via a direct import or within Monday between 2 groups on different boards, I have created a group with 500 tasks and I want to add additional columns with existing data from Excel received from clients in bulk. This is just a VLookUp on excel and is an incredibly basic function that should at least be possible to work around from a copy and paste between boards that are identical in length (number of tasks), where you don’t have to then select each one to add individually. Please please please can you take a look at resolving this?!

Thank you in advance.

posts.author: nealb (Neal Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi
I just installed a vlookup app. It’s in the marketplace
It’s free for I think 3 boards.

posts.author: AlexStanhope (Alex Stanhope)
posts.cookedHtml: ill take a look thank you. Im liaising with Monday.com to try and see if they can add it as a development feature, it seems lik ethey are 90% of the way there they just need to make the import auto populate based on a cell in the existing board.

posts.author: jrobertsaeh (Jim Roberts)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi, I completely agree that this should be offered as a feature. Have you tried update with Excel in the marketplace? I am evaluating now and it seems like it could do what you want but it’s not free.

posts.author: Sergio (Sergio Rodríguez)
posts.cookedHtml: Simple trick to import a new column from Excel, just tried it and works: save your Excel file with the first column to match the item name in Monday and then the new columns you want to add or update. Then start the import new elements process, match the columns and finally check the option to update the item if the item name is the same. It works for me to update a column for a +600 elements table!

posts.author: rstamp (Rebecca Stamp)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agreed to this feature request!
Being able to copy and paste columns into monday from an excel would be game changing. It would save me close to 10+ working hours per month and decrease the amount of “shadow system google sheets” of my Monday boards.

ALSO: If we could get a Copy and paste columns from Monday-- that would save me another 10+ hours per month.
Thanks for all the help team,
Becca

posts.author: b12x12 (Brian Gross)
posts.cookedHtml:
The only way I can think to do this (without manually typing or copying/pasting each item in) would be to export the whole board, make changes in Excel, re-upload. But that get’s really sticky with boards that have lots of different columns of different types, especially since the import feature only allows for text, number, date or status columns… and it resets all the status colours, etc. Gets very complicated to get it to a place where you can then move it all back into the original board to replace everything.

If Monday folks can either add this feature or tell us what makes it not possible to add, that would be great. Maybe some of us could troubleshoot with that information.

posts.author: Keira (Keira)
posts.cookedHtml: I’d also really appreciate this feature. It’s extraordinarily time consuming to add info line by line when I already have it in an excel spreadsheet. It’s a real flaw with this program. (I don’t want to import an entire spreadsheet, just easily copy / paste a list of information, whether text, dates or other info to a specific column in an existing group.

posts.author: AJ302 (AJ Jacobsen)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m exploring project management software for my company, and the lack of this feature working makes this a hard pass for us. Far too time consuming to manually enter everything, too many projects and timelines to use bandaid work arounds.

posts.cookedHtml: I am having the same issue, I will spent hte next 2 hours copying numbers…wish me luck

posts.author: rachel.findlay (Rachel Findlay)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am currently creating a board for resourcing and it would be great if I could copy a column of numbers (from a budget spreadsheet) straight into a numbers column on a board. I know you can copy & paste pulses but couldn’t get this to work for columns of text/numbers.

Note: I don’t want to export the entire excel spreadsheet to create the board just one or two columns of info, hence the import data function wouldn’t work for this.

Thanks,
Rachel

posts.author: Adrian (Adrian F. Guzman)
posts.cookedHtml: YES!!! I want that also! When I was first presenting the Work OS to the team I was like “We can even copy and past info like the task name!” and one coworker said “What about the data pertaining to that task?” I was super excited and jumped the gun on that one when I said “Monday.com can do anything! It can even copy and paste the amounts!” welp…I was wrong

posts.author: ashleyconcierge (Ashley Barrett)
posts.cookedHtml: I had been missing the last step of this update the item if the item name is the same and you SAVED MY LIFE!

posts.author: Emil (Emil)
posts.cookedHtml:
@BiancaT - ANY UPDATE ON THIS?!

I simply need to number my items in 3 boards, all containing over 500 unique items. I thought I had it all figured out when I found the auto numbering column. Sure it works great IN Monday. However if you sort your data first so that the numbers are NOT in order (which is what I want) and then export your board to excel the auto numbering column places all the numbers back in order.

My solution to this, instead of waiting for the auto numbering column to be fixed, was to simply make a number column and import a column with numbers from 1 - 500 but no. there is no option to import a column from excel to an existing board.

What is that?

Please give update and fix, this is such a time-waster to enter it manually.

Thanks.

posts.author: kkovriga (Katerina Kovriga)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi guys,

Our company develops the Smart Spreadsheet for monday app - hope it will help you to use Excel-like features right on monday.com platform.

You’ll be able to import/export your data (and boards with items as well) and work with it as a fully functional spreadsheet (cell formulas, conditional formatting, pivot tables, charts, etc.) in one place.

Best regards,
Katerina Kovriga

posts.author: anakic_querystorm (Antonio Nakic-Alfirevic)
posts.cookedHtml:
I built a plugin for Excel that lets you sync Excel tables and monday boards.

I think a solution that offers 2-way sync between Excel and monday elegantly solves many of the issues listed in this thread.

It makes it easy to move existing Excel data into monday, but also to use Excel functionality for analyzing and editing Monday.com boards (flash fill, find&replace, graphs etc…).

You can also use it to back up boards into Excel files, which have versioning built in (if on one drive).

YouTube video:

Link to web page:

QueryStorm

MondayLink - QueryStorm

Sync Excel tables and Monday.com boards with MondayLink. See what it can do and how to install and use it.

Est. reading time: 10 minutes

It’s been in testing and preview (free for use) for a few months, but it’s a paid product now. The subscription does start with a free trial period, though.

posts.author: amybronstein (Amy Bronstein)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone! Amy here from the monday.com team Thank you so much for your feedback around this feature, we really appreciate you taking the time to let us know what’s important to you!

At this time, this feature isn’t on our long-term roadmap, as we’re currently focused on other high-impact priorities that are further along in development. That said, we’ll continue to evaluate this as we plan ahead.

We’re always listening, and we’ll make sure to share any updates here if anything changes!

M Rad:

posts.author: AnishJ (Anish)

Here’s the link to install the app I was testing it with a few users who DMed me. It’s free for now (currently under review). I tested the app, and it’s very fast copies 2,000 items in just a few seconds. This is a feature I badly wanted for myself.

Now you can copy and paste any specific column really fast. Enjoy and save some time!

posts.author: MRad (M Rad)
posts.cookedHtml: Amazing, Anish! This app could be lifechanging for us and we will be following it closely. I fully understand it’s a brand new idea in it’s early form, if it’s of interest to you my first obstacles with it (copying values out) are excluding null values, excluding rows based on the board view saved filters, and for our use case being unable to use on connected/mirror columns. I will no doubt come up with complicated workarounds in order to still use your apps foundational functions as it is still so useful and unique in the marketplace. Please feel free to contact me if you end up advancing the app and looking for testers. Thanks again

My app is now is live Copy and Paste Column

posts.author: AnishJ (Anish)
posts.cookedHtml: You can now copy Formula and Mirror columns. More features are coming soon, including automations. I’m shipping updates very quickly, so please keep sharing your feedback

posts.author: AnishJ (Anish)
posts.cookedHtml: New update: You can now copy and paste Dropdown columns, and new automation recipes are available (these are missing or not working in other apps).
